title: Luffy Decides to Free Zoro
description: On pages 12–15, this part resolves the Shells Town conflict and separates Coby’s Marine dream from Luffy and Zoro’s pirate path.
scene: In Shells Town, Luffy, Coby, and Zoro are drawn into the cruelty of Morgan’s rule and Helmeppo’s privilege.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: It's clear that you're not friends!!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: DID HE GOAD ME INTO HITTING HIM!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: He did it again!! In the end...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: If I don't depend on myself from now on, then I AM a loser!!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Please let me join the Navy, Sir!!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'll be a cabin boy, or anything, but just let me join!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'm gonna do it!! I'm gonna do it!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: LEUTENANT! I DON'T THINK THAT'S A GOOD IDEA!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: pirates have been known to plant spies in the navy.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: WE SHOULD DO A THOROUGH BACK- GROUND CHECK ON HIM.

summary: The Shells Town conflict uses Zoro’s imprisonment and Morgan’s rule to test what kind of people Luffy chooses to trust. The section moves Zoro closer to becoming Luffy’s first crewmate.
